mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.26% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS above 5.0% depresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1408k units signal robust residential construction demand. Sustained housing construction above 1.3M units indicates strong household formation and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days as construction activity translates to broader economic growth.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) when HOUST > 1.3M versus when HOUST < 1.0M, or correlation between HOUST and subsequent 60-day returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 137.41M and unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.5% indicate a tight labor market. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 50k/month while UNRATE remains stable, this signals weakening labor demand that precedes equity market weakness within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 50k while UNRATE remains within 4.0-5.0%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following the trigger condition are positive in more than 60% of instances, or the joint signal (PAYEMS deceleration + stable UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 111.93 reflects current manufacturing capacity utilization. A decline in INDPRO of more than 0.5% month-over-month signals deteriorating supply-side momentum and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 370.5B and real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 11506.5B jointly capture consumer and aggregate demand strength. When RSAFS growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month concurrent with positive GDPC1 growth, equity valuations expand due to earnings growth expectations, producing positive 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth exceeds 0.5% and GDPC1 growth is positive
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 203.15 combined with FEDFUNDS at 5.26% indicates the Fed is in active tightening mode. When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus inflation growth) exceeds 2.5%, equity risk premiums rise sharply, producing negative 20-day returns as investors demand higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus 3-month CPI growth rate) exceeds 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where real rates exceed 2.5%, or the interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.5% is near cyclical lows. When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, this signals labor market deterioration that precedes equity market weakness, producing negative 20-day returns as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following UNRATE spikes > 0.3pp are positive in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1408k and retail sales (RSAFS) at 370.5B jointly measure consumer-side demand. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, this signals a synchronized demand shock that produces negative 5-day equity returns as forward earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS decline more than 2% month-over-month in the same month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 2%, or the joint signal Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
